channel.title: Drones on Dyson Sphere Program Guide
channel.description: Recent content in Drones on Dyson Sphere Program Guide

item.title: How to Fix ILS Not Picking Up Items From DSP Local Storage
item.description:
You have 2,000 iron ingots sitting inside the ILS storage. The drones are idle. Nothing is moving. Your factory downstream is starving. This is the most common ILS troubleshooting issue in DSP.

TL;DR — The Fix:
Open the ILS panel. Set the slot to Supply (not Storage). Remote Demand set to 0. Local Demand set to 0 or your target. Drones will now pick up from this station.
The golden rule: "Supply" means drones can TAKE items out. "Storage" means items just sit there.

The #1 Culprit: Slot Mode Mismatch

The ILS has three slot states per item type: Supply, Demand, and Storage. These are NOT the same as the station-level "Local Supply / Local Demand / Remote Supply / Remote Demand" settings. They are per-slot behaviors that many players confuse.

item.title: How to Prevent DSP Logistic Drone Congestion at ILS Station
item.description:
You have 200 drones orbiting a single ILS while three other stations sit empty. Items that should take 10 seconds to move take 5 minutes. Your factory is running at 30% because of logistics gridlock. Drone congestion is the hidden throughput killer of DSP.

TL;DR — The Fix:
Cap your drones per ILS station (5-10 max), use priority splitters to balance material distribution, and add orbital buffer chests near high-traffic buildings.
The magic number: 8 drones per ILS station is the sweet spot for 95% of factory setups.

What Causes Drone Gridlock

The drone congestion problem is a classic tragedy of the commons in DSP's logistics system. Here is the chain reaction:
